title: Growth Energy's Letter to California Senator Catherine Blakespear Urging Support for AB 30
content: Growth Energy, representing 97 U.S. biorefineries that produce over nine billion gallons of renewable fuel annually, supports Californiaâ€™s AB 30 (Alvarez), which would legalize the sale and use of E15â€”a gasoline blend containing up to 15% ethanol. The organization emphasizes that E15 offers economic and environmental benefits, potentially saving California drivers up to $0.20 per gallon and $2.7 billion annually if E15 replaces E10. The adoption of E15 would also diversify the stateâ€™s energy mix, lower transportation fuel costs, and advance decarbonization goals. In addition to cost savings, E15 has been proven to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and harmful particulates. Research by the University of California, Riverside shows that E15 lowers tailpipe emissions compared to E10. Approved by the U.S. EPA in 2011 for all gasoline vehicles model year 2001 and newer, E15 has powered over 150 billion miles nationwide and is now available in 34 states. Growth Energy urges California to approve AB 30 to align with national standards and enhance affordability, air quality, and energy sustainability.
author: Analysts Account
